O.label: Operator
O.description: Entity operating or controlling the automated decision system.

E.label: Engine
E.description: Decision engine or runtime being monitored.

I.label: Input / Observed State
I.description: State, data, event, or boundary observed by the sensor.

K.label: Sensor Signing Key
K.description: Key used by the sensor to sign or authenticate its readings.

R.label: Receipt
R.description: Receipt or record containing sensor output.

A.label: External Anchor
A.description: Timestamp, ledger, or other external anchoring mechanism.

PKD.label: Public Key Directory
PKD.description: Directory or metadata source used to retrieve public keys, certificates, or verification material.

P.label: Affected Party
P.description: Person or entity affected by the decision.

V.label: Verifier
V.description: Party or tool attempting to verify the sensor output.

Reg.label: Regulator or Independent Reviewer
Reg.description: External party able to inspect or reconstruct evidence under defined assumptions.

scenario_a_operator_controlled_internal_.description: Operator controls the engine, sensor, input, receipt channel, and verification access.

expected_conservative_result.reason: The sensor, input, receipt, and verification access are controlled by the operator.
expected_conservative_result.ndc_effect: Apparent sensor path collapses into the operator domain. Conservative NDC remains 1.
expected_conservative_result.surviving_claim: No materially independent sensor claim survives.

scenario_b_operator_controlled_sensor_ke.description: Sensor signs its readings, but the operator controls the signing key or can cause the key to be used.

expected_conservative_result.reason: A signature does not create independence when the operator controls the signing key.
expected_conservative_result.ndc_effect: Sensor and key collapse into the operator domain. Conservative NDC remains 1.

scenario_c_protected_sensor_operator_con.description: Sensor runs in a protected environment with non-exportable key, but operator controls the input or observation boundary.

expected_conservative_result.reason: The protected sensor may faithfully sign a state that was already distorted by the operator-controlled input or observation boundary.
expected_conservative_result.ndc_effect: For origin, input truth, and completeness, the cut still passes through operator-controlled input. For post-observation tamper evidence, a narrow integrity claim may survive.
expected_conservative_result.surviving_claim: A narrow claim that the observed state was signed and anchored after observation, not that the observed state was true or complete.

scenario_d1_disjoint_sensor_affected_par.description: Sensor key custody, observation boundary, anchoring path, public-key directory, and receipt possession are materially disjoint from the operator.

expected_conservative_result.reason: Key custody, observation boundary, anchor, public-key metadata, and receipt possession are materially outside operator control.
expected_conservative_result.ndc_effect: Sensor may survive as an independent verification path for the specific measured claim.
expected_conservative_result.surviving_claim: The sensor may support an independent claim about what it measured, subject to the defined observation boundary.

scenario_d2_disjoint_sensor_non_exclusiv.description: Sensor key custody and anchoring are disjoint, but receipt access occurs through a non-exclusive channel that may still be influenced by the operator.

expected_conservative_result.reason: A non-exclusive channel is not automatically equivalent to affected-party possession if the operator can suppress, delay, gate, or selectively disclose evidence.
expected_conservative_result.ndc_effect: The min-cut must be evaluated separately. Do not report D2 as D1.
expected_conservative_result.surviving_claim: Only survives if receipt access is practically reachable outside operator suppression or gating.
